actionable_feedback: Add an explicit sensitivity analysis that applies sensitivity_shift_pct (5%) to the relevant input metrics and shows how normalized_risk and classification change. Provide both (a) an SQL snippet that computes the sensitivity-shifted normalized_risk for each record and (b) the same computations in the spreadsheet.

Ensure cohort_qc.xlsx contains visible cell formulas for the key computed columns (observable_partial_risk, normalized_risk, classification) or export those computed columns as a CSV alongside the workbook so parsers can validate reproducibility.

Fix workbook compatibility so openpyxl can parse cohort_qc.xlsx (avoid macros or exotic formats; save as a standard .xlsx). Include a small README or metadata sheet that documents which cells hold which formulas and the sheet names.

Add an explicit cost rationale tying the recommended intervention(s) for warned/failed records to decision_budget_usd (e.g., estimated staff-hours  hourly rate or an estimated percent of decision_budget_usd) so reviewers can assess whether interventions are affordable.
Document boundary handling and inclusivity rules for thresholds (e.g., whether >= 0.95 is inclusive for 'warn'; confirm exact behavior in both SQL and spreadsheet comments).

If the missing limit_logic_leakage_risk can be supplied, add an alternate computational branch (or example) showing final numeric normalized_risk and resulting classifications when that limit is present; otherwise keep the explicit 'needs_review' logic but show how results would change if a plausible limit were provided.

feedback_summary: Submission correctly includes required artifacts and defensibly handles a missing denominator by marking records 'needs_review'. Partial credit was assigned because (1) sensitivity analysis using sensitivity_shift_pct is not demonstrated, (2) cohort_qc.xlsx could not be programmatically inspected (openpyxl unavailable) so spreadsheet-visible formulas and per-cell reproducibility could not be validated, and (3) the decision rationale omits explicit cost estimates tied to decision_budget_usd and handling of boundary cases.

grader_summary.reason: Artifacts present; SQL handles missing denominator by marking 'needs_review'. Missing sensitivity analysis and unverifiable spreadsheet formulas justify partial credit.
